forecasts.reasoning: The State of Emergency (SOE) for Samoa was declared on March 31, 2025, and explicitly set to expire on April 29, 2025. It was primarily enacted due to a severe power crisis caused by generator failures. Temporary generators arrived on April 5, and as of April 20, 2025, the Electricity Power Corporation of Samoa officially canceled the regular power rationing program on Upolu, stating that generation capacity now meets demand. Because the immediate crisis triggering the SOE has effectively been resolved (as evidenced by the return to normal electricity distribution), the government has no functional reason to extend the state of emergency beyond its scheduled April 29 conclusion, nor is there any evidence of an alternate emergency declaration leading up to the April 21 knowledge cutoff. There remains a small risk that the SOE is nominally extended for administrative reasons (e.g. to maintain tax exemptions on energy imports until permanent generators arrive in August), but standard practice in returning to regular power distribution strongly suggests it will be allowed to lapse before April 30.

Rep. Haley Stevens has taken significant steps toward launching a Senate campaign for the open Michigan seat. On April 10, Punchbowl News reported she told fellow Democrats she would launch her campaign "imminently," and she recently hired former DSCC Executive Director Christie Roberts as a campaign consultant. Her earlier timeline (from a February Axios report) indicated she planned to make her decision in April. With Easter 2025 falling on April 20, a launch is incredibly likely to happen in the days immediately following the holiday—meaning the last full week of April. Her hiring of top-tier staff, leaks of an "imminent launch", and the remaining 10 days until May 1 give us extremely high confidence that she will officially announce before the resolution date.
